summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates filesystem and git history to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes shell pipelines via `/bin/sh -c` that recursively grep, find, and cat files under /app and dump git history across all branches/commits. The harvested content (including matches for HTB{...} CTF flag patterns and arbitrary file contents) is PUT as JSON to hardcoded endpoints including the remote IP http://154.57.164.71:31289/api/modules/ECT-472839. Execution is gated by victim-fingerprinting: it requires a CTF-shaped 12-hex-character hostname with /app cwd or an internal verdaccio/nexus registry, and explicitly skips /tmp and AWS/Hetzner hosts to evade sandboxes. The shell helper uses `execFileSync("/bin/sh", ["-c", cmd], {maxBuffer: 10MB})` to support large captures. The same machinery harvests source code, commit history, and file contents from any installer matching the gate.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
